name: Cretan Bull
description: The Cretan Bull was a magnificent and powerful bull in Greek mythology. Its parentage varies in different accounts; some say it was sent by Poseidon to King Minos of Crete, while others claim it was the offspring of Pasiphae, Minos's wife, and a bull sent by Poseidon. Minos was supposed to sacrifice the bull to Poseidon but, finding it too beautiful, he substituted another. Enraged, Poseidon caused Pasiphae to fall in love with the bull, resulting in the birth of the Minotaur. The Cretan Bull was known for its immense strength, ferocity, and wild nature. It ravaged the Cretan countryside, causing widespread destruction and terror. Eventually, Heracles was tasked with capturing the bull as one of his twelve labors. He successfully subdued the beast and brought it back to mainland Greece. After Heracles completed his task, the bull was set loose again, eventually making its way to Marathon where it was eventually killed by Theseus. The Cretan Bull represents untamed power, divine wrath, and the challenges faced by heroes in their quests to restore order and balance to the world. Its capture and subsequent defeat highlight the triumph of human strength and cunning over bestial fury.
subtitle: A raging beast of Crete.
appearance: The Cretan Bull is depicted as a massive and powerful bull, typically pure white in color, though some accounts describe it as being tawny or black. Its horns are exceptionally large and sharp, capable of causing significant damage. It possesses a muscular build, radiating raw power and untamed energy. Its eyes burn with a fierce intensity, reflecting its wild and aggressive nature. Its hooves are said to be as hard as stone, allowing it to traverse any terrain with ease.
habitat: Originally the island of Crete, specifically the plains and countryside. After being captured by Heracles, it roamed mainland Greece, eventually settling near Marathon.
behavior: The Cretan Bull is characterized by its wild and uncontrollable nature. It is fiercely territorial and prone to fits of rage, often charging at anything that moves. It is destructive and causes chaos wherever it goes. It is also depicted as being incredibly stubborn and difficult to control, making its capture a challenging feat.

keyMyths.title: Heracles' Seventh Labor
keyMyths.description: Heracles was tasked with capturing the Cretan Bull as his seventh labor. After a fierce struggle, he managed to subdue the bull and transport it back to Mycenae, proving his strength and completing his task.

keyMyths.title: Theseus and the Bull of Marathon
keyMyths.description: After being released, the Cretan Bull wandered to Marathon, where it terrorized the local population. Theseus eventually confronted and killed the bull, further establishing his reputation as a hero.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive compilation of Greek myths and legends.
sources.text: A comprehensive compilation of Greek myths and legends.
